criterion: Stop concurrent Kotlin LSP instances from racing — either serialize them via a mux or document a stable single-instance policy.

acceptance_signals.description: Reproduction recorded: two project activations on the same Kotlin source tree spawn rival kotlin-lsp instances
acceptance_signals.evidence: Repro captured in docs/issues/2026-03-24-kotlin-lsp-concurrent-instances.md — opening kotlin-library twice deadlocks both instances on the build/ lock.

acceptance_signals.description: Decision recorded: serialize behind a mux, or hold a single shared instance per workspace
acceptance_signals.evidence: ADR: share one kotlin-lsp instance keyed by workspace root; the LSP mux dispatches requests by project id.

acceptance_signals.description: Cold-start circuit-breaker covers Kotlin path (no infinite retry on startup failure)
Integration test covers concurrent activation of two Kotlin projects

children.title: Reproduce Kotlin LSP race in fixtures/kotlin-library
Pool capacity bump 5 → 10 for worktree swarms

progress_log.note: Issue filed — concurrent kotlin-lsp instances deadlock on build/ lock when two project activations land within a few seconds.
Mux dispatcher landed. Single kotlin-lsp per workspace root keyed in the LSP pool. Worktree swarms still fight for the 5-slot default.
Bumped LSP pool capacity 5 → 10 to absorb worktree swarms.
